title: Your First LLM Fine-tune on Solo Hub
description: End-to-end flow: account setup → credits → dataset prep → 6-step wizard → monitoring. Available on all plans including Basic.

check_account.instruction: Do you already have a Solo Hub account?

create_account.instruction: Go to hub.getsolo.tech, click Sign Up, complete the profile onboarding. Fill in all required fields and accept Terms & Code of Conduct.

on_failure.recovery: Try refreshing — if you signed up via a shared link, Hub auto-redirects back

check_email_verified.instruction: Is your email verified? (Check — is there a verification banner at the top of Hub?)

verify_email.instruction: Click 'Resend Verification Email' in the top banner, check your inbox, and click the link. You need this to access billing.

on_failure.recovery: Check spam folder. Wait 2-3 minutes and try resending. Confirm email address in Settings → Profile.

check_credits.instruction: Look at your credit balance in the top navbar. Do you have credits?

buy_credits.instruction: Go to Settings → Billing → Buy Credits. The Bronze pack ($10, 50 credits) is a safe starting point for your first job.

on_failure.recovery: You need Admin role on your org. Or email not verified — check both.

prepare_dataset.instruction: Do you have your training dataset ready on HuggingFace? (You need: the repo ID, and the exact column name containing your training text)

dataset_guidance.instruction:
Your dataset needs to be on HuggingFace in one of two formats:
1. A 'messages' column with list-of-turn conversation objects (preferred)
2. Paired columns like instruction+response or question+answer

Upload your dataset to HuggingFace at huggingface.co/new-dataset. Then come back here.

use_example_dataset.instruction:
Solo provides example datasets you can use to test the wizard:
- GetSoloTech/Math-Reasoning (math Q&A) — Text Field: messages
- GetSoloTech/Code-Reasoning (code tasks) — Text Field: messages

For Math-Reasoning: Dataset = GetSoloTech/Math-Reasoning, Text Field = messages

wizard_step1_type.instruction: Open the fine-tuning wizard in Solo Hub. On Step 1, click 'LLM' (brain icon).

on_failure.message: This is expected if you previously had VLA selected and are on Basic. Click LLM button explicitly.

wizard_step1_model.instruction: Select a base model. Llama-3.1-8B (marked Recommended) is the best starting point for most tasks.

on_failure.recovery: Ensure you clicked LLM type first — model list only appears after type is selected

wizard_step2_gpu.instruction: Select H100 80GB (Recommended). It finishes faster, burning fewer total credits despite the higher per-minute rate. Do NOT select A10 24GB — it is marked Insufficient VRAM.

on_failure.recovery: This GPU has insufficient VRAM for LLM fine-tuning. Select A100 40GB or H100 80GB.

wizard_step3_params.instruction: Set training parameters. For your first run, use the recommended values: Epochs=3, Max Steps=1000, LoRA Rank=16, Batch Size=8, LoRA Alpha=32, Gradient Accumulation=4.

on_failure.recovery: Use the recommended values shown in the wizard UI if uncertain

wizard_step4_data.instruction: Connect your dataset. Enter the HuggingFace repo ID (format: owner/dataset-name). CRITICAL: verify the Text Field value exactly matches the column name in your dataset. A mismatch is the #1 cause of LLM job failures.
wizard_step4_data.validation_prompt: Before proceeding: go to your dataset page on HuggingFace and confirm the column name you entered as Text Field matches exactly.

on_failure.recovery: Open your dataset on HuggingFace, look at the actual column names in the dataset viewer, and correct the Text Field value

wizard_step5_integrations.instruction: Do you want to push the trained model to HuggingFace Hub or use W&B tracking?

configure_hf_push.instruction: Toggle on 'Push to HuggingFace Hub'. Enter your HF token (format: hf_xxx) and repo name (format: username/model-name). Ensure token has write access.

on_failure.recovery: Generate a new token at huggingface.co/settings/tokens with write permission

configure_wandb.instruction: Toggle on 'Weights & Biases'. Enter your W&B API token. Ensure your W&B project exists before launching.

on_failure.error: Invalid W&B token or project missing
on_failure.recovery: Verify token at wandb.ai/settings. Create the target project in W&B first.

wizard_step6_launch.instruction: Review the cost estimate panel. Note: burn rate (credits/hr), per-minute cost, and your max runtime. Credits deduct from Provisioning onward — job auto-stops at zero balance. When satisfied, click 'Start Training'.
wizard_step6_launch.validation_prompt: After clicking Start Training: do you see a results card with a Job ID and status = QUEUED?

error_map.Start Training disabled: Check plan permissions; verify you're logged in to correct org
error_map.Insufficient credits: Add credits via Settings → Billing → Buy Credits
error_map.Job already exists: Change the Output Model Name to something unique
error_map.Maximum concurrent jobs: Wait for an existing job to reach Completed/Failed/Cancelled

monitor_job.instruction: Click the model link in the results card. Open the Diagnostics tab. You'll see the pipeline move through: QUEUED → Provisioning → Initializing → Training → Uploading → Completed. Credits start at Provisioning.
monitor_job.validation_prompt: Is the job visible in the Diagnostics tab? What stage is it currently in?

on_failure.error: Job stopped at Provisioning or Initializing

dataset_failure_triage.instruction: Did the job fail? What type of error appeared in Diagnostics?

fix_text_field.instruction: Go back to your HuggingFace dataset page and verify the exact column name. Open the Dataset Viewer and check the column headers. Then re-launch the wizard with the corrected Text Field value.

fix_hf_credentials.instruction: Go to huggingface.co/settings/tokens. Generate a new token with write scope. Update the token in the wizard's integrations step and re-launch.

tutorial_complete.instruction: Your LLM fine-tuning job has completed. Your model is now in your Solo Hub org's catalog. Open the model's detail page → Usage tab to see how to run inference.
